title: Rewrite Ecto search context to remove SQL injection
prompt: `test_fixtures/ecto_fragment_search.ex` contains three Ecto queries with SQL injection vulnerabilities: a search by name, a date filter, and a `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query` escape hatch. Rewrite the entire module (`MyApp.Catalog`) so: (1) `search_products/1` uses `fragment("... ILIKE ?", ^like)` with the `%`-wrapped term built at the Elixir level, (2) `products_created_after/1` uses `p.inserted_at > ^date` with a proper `DateTime` or `NaiveDateTime` input, and (3) `find_by_id/1` uses the Ecto query builder instead of `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query`. Preserve all three public function names. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app/catalog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Three related injections in one file; the canonical fixes are well-known. Easy because the patterns are mechanical, but requires fixing all three to score.
